agent_output: {"executive_summary":"A 9x increase in P99 latency (200ms→1800ms) over two weeks, with normal DB query times, no errors, and no deployment trigger, strongly points to external API degradation — most likely one of the three external dependencies (geocoding, carrier rates, or payment gateway) is experiencing progressive slowdown. The gradual onset without errors rules out code regressions and suggests either upstream service degradation, connection pool exhaustion from slow-draining connections, or DNS resolution issues. The most probable root cause is one external API's response time increasing gradually (possibly due to rate limiting, upstream capacity issues, or changed routing), compounded by missing or misconfigured timeouts that allow slow responses to cascade.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Logistics API Service","responsibility":"Orchestrates shipment operations by coordinating geocoding, carrier rate calculation, and payment processing","boundaries":"Application boundary — owns business logic, delegates to external APIs"},{"name":"Geocoding Service (External)","responsibility":"Resolves addresses to coordinates","boundaries":"External dependency — third-party API"},{"name":"Carrier Rates API (External)","responsibility":"Provides shipping rate quotes from carriers","boundaries":"External dependency — third-party API"},{"name":"Payment Gateway (External)","responsibility":"Processes payments for shipments","boundaries":"External dependency — third-party API"},{"name":"Database","responsibility":"Persists shipment, customer, and transaction data","boundaries":"Internal infrastructure — query times confirmed normal"}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Geocoding Service","contract":"HTTP REST — address in, coordinates out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Carrier Rates API","contract":"HTTP REST — origin/destination/weight in, rate quotes out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Payment Gateway","contract":"HTTP REST — payment details in, confirmation out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Database","contract":"SQL queries via connection pool","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous, confirmed healthy)"}],"data_flow":"Inbound request → (potentially sequential) calls to geocoding → carrier rates → payment gateway → DB write → response. If any external call is sequential, total latency = sum of all call latencies. P99 at 1800ms suggests at least one call adding ~1600ms.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Sequential External API Orchestration (current, suspected)","rationale":"The latency profile (1800ms P99) suggests calls are made sequentially, so one slow dependency adds directly to total latency"},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker (missing, recommended)","rationale":"No errors reported despite degradation — the system lacks circuit breakers that would fail fast on slow upstream services"},{"pattern":"Timeout Configuration (likely misconfigured)","rationale":"Gradual degradation without errors means the service waits indefinitely (or with generous timeouts) for slow responses"}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Shipment Orchestration","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment","Quote","Route"]},{"name":"Geocoding (External)","classification":"generic","aggregates":[]},{"name":"Carrier Rating (External)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":[]},{"name":"Payment (External)","classification":"generic","aggregates":[]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Geocoding Service","downstream":"Logistics API","relationship":"Conformist — service accepts whatever the geocoding API returns"},{"upstream":"Carrier Rates API","downstream":"Logistics API","relationship":"Conformist — service conforms to carrier API contract"},{"upstream":"Payment Gateway","downstream":"Logistics API","relationship":"Conformist — service conforms to payment API contract"}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Synchronous orchestration of external APIs","gains":"Simple implementation, strong consistency, easy to reason about","sacrifices":"Latency is sum of all calls; one slow dependency degrades the entire request","alternatives_considered":["Parallel external calls (reduces latency to max of all calls)","Async processing with webhook callbacks","Caching geocoding results to eliminate redundant calls"]},{"decision":"No circuit breakers on external calls","gains":"Simpler code, no false-positive circuit trips","sacrifices":"Slow dependencies silently degrade the entire service with no protection","alternatives_considered":["Circuit breaker per external dependency","Bulkhead isolation per dependency","Timeout + retry with exponential backoff"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Geocoding results caching","evolution_path":"Geocoding results are highly cacheable (address→coordinates rarely change). Adding a cache here could eliminate one external call entirely for repeated addresses."},{"area":"Parallel external API calls","evolution_path":"If geocoding, rates, and payment are independent within a request, parallelize them. If dependent (geocoding feeds into rates), parallelize where possible."},{"area":"Timeout tuning","evolution_path":"Add aggressive per-dependency timeouts (e.g., 500ms for geocoding, 1s for rates, 2s for payment) with fallback behavior."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"External API contracts","reason":"No control over third-party API performance or contract changes; must design defensively around them"},{"area":"Sequential call ordering","reason":"If business logic requires geocoding before rate calculation, that sequential dependency is inherent"}],"risks":[{"risk":"Hypothesis 1 (MOST LIKELY): One external API is degrading gradually — carrier rates APIs commonly slow down as they aggregate more carrier responses or hit rate limits based on growing usage patterns","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Instrument per-dependency latency tracking (not just aggregate). Add distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry) to identify exactly which call is slow. Check external API status pages and contact providers."},{"risk":"Hypothesis 2 (LIKELY): Connection pool exhaustion — slow external responses hold connections longer, reducing available pool capacity, causing queuing for subsequent requests. This creates a positive feedback loop where slowness begets more slowness.","severity":"high","mitigation":"Check HTTP client connection pool metrics. Look for increasing queue wait times. Verify pool sizes and idle connection timeouts. Check if the HTTP client reuses connections or creates new ones."},{"risk":"Hypothesis 3 (MODERATE): DNS resolution degradation — if DNS TTLs are short and the resolver is slow, every external API call pays a DNS penalty that wouldn't show up in DB dashboards","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Check DNS resolution times from the service host. Verify DNS caching is enabled. Check if /etc/resolv.conf or DNS provider changed."},{"risk":"Hypothesis 4 (MODERATE): TLS handshake overhead — if TLS session resumption broke or certificates changed, each external call pays full TLS handshake cost","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Check TLS handshake times in connection metrics. Verify TLS session caching is working. Check if any external API changed certificates."},{"risk":"Hypothesis 5 (LOW-MODERATE): Memory pressure / GC pauses — gradual memory leak causing increasingly frequent GC pauses that add latency without errors","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Check heap usage trends over the two-week period. Look for sawtooth patterns with increasing baseline. Check GC pause logs."},{"risk":"Hypothesis 6 (LOW): Payload size growth — if responses from external APIs are growing (e.g., more carrier options, more geocoding candidates), serialization/deserialization time increases","severity":"low","mitigation":"Log response payload sizes over time. Check if carrier rates API is returning more options than before."},{"risk":"Missing observability — the team cannot currently distinguish which external dependency is slow, which is the most critical gap","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Add per-dependency latency histograms immediately. This is the single highest-value action."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Three-phase diagnostic and remediation: (1) IMMEDIATE: Add per-dependency latency instrumentation to identify which external API is slow. Add request-level tracing. This will confirm or refute Hypothesis 1 within hours. (2) SHORT-TERM: Add timeouts (500ms geocoding, 1s carrier, 2s payment), circuit breakers per dependency, and geocoding result caching. (3) MEDIUM-TERM: Parallelize independent external calls, add bulkhead isolation, implement fallback strategies (cached rates, degraded geocoding).","files_to_create_or_modify":["middleware/request-tracing.{ext} — distributed tracing middleware","clients/geocoding-client.{ext} — add timeout, circuit breaker, response caching","clients/carrier-rates-client.{ext} — add timeout, circuit breaker","clients/payment-client.{ext} — add timeout, circuit breaker","config/timeouts.{ext} — centralized timeout configuration","monitoring/dependency-health.{ext} — per-dependency health dashboard config"],"patterns_to_follow":"Circuit Breaker pattern (Hystrix/Resilience4j style) per external dependency. Bulkhead pattern to isolate thread/connection pools per dependency. Cache-aside pattern for geocoding. Timeout + retry with exponential backoff and jitter.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT add retries without timeouts — retrying a slow call makes things worse","Do NOT set a single global timeout — each dependency has different latency profiles","Do NOT cache payment or carrier rate responses without careful TTL consideration (stale rates = revenue risk)","Do NOT add circuit breakers that fail open without a fallback strategy","Do NOT blame the database just because it is internal — the evidence already exonerates it"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["External API degradation is the root cause category — DB is exonerated, no deployment trigger rules out code regression, gradual onset fits upstream degradation","Missing per-dependency observability is the critical gap preventing diagnosis","Sequential external calls amplify single-dependency slowdowns"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which specific external API is degrading — cannot determine without instrumentation (carrier rates API is most likely based on industry patterns)","Whether connection pool exhaustion is a compounding factor — requires metrics to confirm","Whether the three external calls are sequential or partially parallel — assumed sequential based on latency profile"],"assumptions":["Database dashboard metrics are accurate and comprehensive","The three external API calls happen in the request hot path (not async/background)","No infrastructure changes (network, DNS, load balancer) occurred in the two-week window","10K requests/day is roughly uniform distribution, not bursty"]}}
